item.title: Fed’s Daly Says Statement Language Is Less Important Than Action
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly downplayed division among policymakers over the central bank’s policy statement, signaling she wouldn’t have dissented against it as some of her voting colleagues did.

item.title: Schnabel Says ECB Will Need to Hike If Energy Shock Broadens
item.description: The European Central Bank will have to raise interest rates if the Iran war leaves a more lasting mark on inflation, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

item.title: Mexico Plans $8 Billion in Gas Pipelines to Boost Power Sector
item.description: Mexico is planning to invest 140 billion pesos ($8.1 billion) in new gas pipelines over the next four years, the latest step in President Claudia Sheinbaum’s plan to boost the country’s power generation.

item.title: Organic Juice Maker Suja Life Sinks 14% After $186.7 Million IPO
item.description: Suja Life Inc. shares fell 14% in its trading debut on Thursday, after the initial public offering for the maker of organic cold-pressed juices and other nutritional beverages raised $186.7 million.

item.title: Bank of England to Pay First Dividend to Treasury Since Pandemic
item.description: The Bank of England will resume paying a dividend to the UK government as it starts to bring its finances under control.

item.title: WEF Should Be Led by Person Without Corporate Links, Schwab Says
item.description: The World Economic Forum should be led by someone who doesn’t have a corporate background, according to its founder and former chairman Klaus Schwab, in an apparent dig at the organization’s current leadership.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Jumps 30% After $416 Million IPO
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc. soared 30% after the provider of satellite-based signals intelligence for US government agencies raised $416 million in a US initial public offering priced at the top of a marketed range.

item.title: Citadel Securities Clearing Own Stock Options in Break From BofA
item.description: Citadel Securities is taking a do-it-yourself approach to clearing its equity options trades, bringing an end to one part of a business relationship with Bank of America Corp. going back a quarter century.

item.title: BlackRock South Korea ETF Sees Record Exodus as AI Stocks Soar
item.description: Investors pulled cash out of a BlackRock exchange-traded fund tracking South Korean equities at the fastest pace on record on Wednesday amid a broad-based rally that has taken the nation’s stock index to all-time highs.

item.title: Arm Sees ‘Explosion’ in CPU Demand on AI Amid Smartphone Slump
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc Chief Executive Officer Rene Haas said the company is experiencing an “explosion of demand” for its CPU architecture driven by AI workloads in data centers, with orders doubling to $2 billion in just five weeks.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Jump for Second Week to 6.37%, Freddie Mac Says
item.description: Mortgage rates in the US jumped for the second straight week, a rise in borrowing costs that threatens to stall the spring home sales season.

item.title: Polish Drone Maker Eyes Significant Growth From EU Defense Loans
item.description: Polish producer of military drones WB Electronics sees the European Union’s loans-for-weapons program as a “significant growth factor” for the company that’s considering an initial public offering.

item.title: TD’s eBay Financing Assumes GameStop Wins Longshot Credit Rating
item.description: GameStop Corp.’s proposed $20 billion financing of its bid for eBay Inc. rests on the assumption that the combined company’s debt would be investment grade, a premise that people familiar with the matter and at least one ratings firm suggest is far from a sure thing.

item.title: Oil Market Liquidity Dries Up as Traders Sit Out War Volatility
item.description: Oil market liquidity has evaporated since the start of the US-Iran war, amplifying price moves in a benchmark seen as a bellwether for the global economy.

item.title: Ethiopia Inflation Back in Double Digits on Biting Fuel Shortage
item.description: Ethiopia’s inflation rate is back above 10% for the first time in five months, after fuel supply challenges pushed up the cost of almost everything.

item.title: IMF Warns Demand for Angola’s Bonds to Cool If Iran War Drags On
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said demand for Angola’s bonds will wane if the Iran war continues, putting pressure on inflation and the southern African nation’s currency amid higher import costs.

item.title: Venezuela Floods Market With Dollars to Ease Inflation Pressure
item.description: Venezuela’s government is stepping up dollar sales and allowing the bolivar to weaken, narrowing the gap with the parallel market and easing pressure on inflation.

item.title: Israeli Shekel at 32-Year High Is Causing Angst in Tech Industry
item.description: Despite global condemnation over Israel’s regional wars, the shekel is at a 32-year high, giving the government something to brag about, but fueling anxiety in the vital tech industry.

item.title: Short-Term Inflation Expectations Edge Up, New York Fed Survey Finds
item.description: US consumers’ near-term inflation expectations inched higher in April, while the long-term price outlook remained stable, according to a Federal Reserve Bank of New York survey.

item.title: I Squared, Blackstone Eye €2.5 Billion Stroeer Buyout
item.description: I Squared and Blackstone Inc. are edging closer to making a bid for Stroeer SE & Co. that could value the German media group at about €2.5 billion ($2.9 billion), people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Boosted Logistics Spending to Blunt Impact of War
item.description: Saudi Arabia brought forward some planned spending on infrastructure and food imports to soften the impact of the Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a move that added to its budget deficit in the first quarter.

item.title: Hungary Looks to June for Potential Rate Move on Strong Forint
item.description: The forint’s post-election rally should give the central bank an opportunity to reassess its cautious monetary policy, Vice Governor Zoltan Kurali said, signaling that an interest-rate cut as early as June could be on the cards.

item.title: Merz Slams UniCredit for Destroying Trust With Commerzbank Bid
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz issued a blunt criticism of UniCredit SpA’s bid for Commerzbank AG, saying the takeover effort destroys trust.

item.title: Fed’s Hammack Says FOMC Signal on Rate Path Was Misleading
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said language in policymakers’ post-meeting statement suggesting the central bank’s next move would be a cut didn’t match her outlook.

item.title: Fed Needs Policy Clarity to Preserve Independence, G-30 Report Says
item.description: The Federal Reserve needs to improve its execution of monetary policy and how it communicates with investors and the public if it is to protect its independence against ongoing political attacks, according to a think tank of former and current policymakers.

item.title: The Messy, Humiliating Courtroom Drama Between Elon Musk and OpenAI
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies were already unpopular before the billionaires started fighting in court.

item.title: Kushner-Backed Israeli Firm Moves to Defend Its Brightline Bonds
item.description: An Israel-based firm has amassed a sizable stake in the corporate bonds of Florida’s Brightline rail project and is gearing up for workout talks, as the cash-strapped venture struggles under its debt pile.

item.title: Centuries-Old UK Tin Mine Sells 13.5% Bonds to Fund Revival
item.description: A bid to restart tin mining in southwestern England, reviving an industry with roots in the Bronze Age, has won a $210 million vote of confidence from bond buyers.

item.title: PE Firm Hg Marks Down Fund Value by 9% After Software’s Plunge
item.description: Private equity firm Hg marked down the portfolio value of one of its funds by 9% in the first quarter after software valuations fell to a 20-year low, highlighting concerns that artificial intelligence will deal a huge blow to the industry.

item.title: Google Launches $100 Fitbit Air Without a Screen to Rival Whoop
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google on Thursday launched the Fitbit Air, a $100 screenless fitness band aimed squarely at taking on Whoop Inc. and other makers of health and fitness wearables that forgo displays.
